_doc: Trace B — THE WOW MOMENT (Invariant 2). Issued ~90s after Trace A. Recall surfaces the planted retry-budget DM that Trace A's recall did NOT — that's the visible reinforcement signal. Tool-call latencies are deliberately faster than Trace A: 'second triage is faster' is falsifiable in delay_ms.

memories.text: Stripe webhook flaking, investigating duplicate charge events from cus_** — looks like the retry path hits processCharge twice when our 200 ack is slow.
Postmortem 2024-01-14 — Stripe webhook regression. Root cause: handler does not check idempotency on retried webhook deliveries.
ADR-007 — Idempotency keys for Stripe webhooks. lib/idempotency.ts.has() must be called before any side-effecting processCharge/processRefund.
DM oncall→eng-lead, Feb 18: 'we should add a retry budget on idempotency keys, this'll bite us again — both processCharge AND processRefund use the same hot path and neither is wrapped'.

User triaged idempotency-key incident in webhooks/stripe.ts — reinforced this cluster. Reinforced memory_ids: [mem_slk_jan14_thread_001, mem_ntn_pm_2024_01_14, mem_ntn_adr_007]. Triage outcome: suspected_fix at webhooks/stripe.ts:84 (idempotency check missing on retry).

snippets.content: Every Stripe webhook handler MUST consult a persistent idempotency check on event.id before performing any side effect, on every code path including retry paths.

recent_commits.author: eng-lead
recent_commits.message: fix(stripe): add retry handling to processWebhook (does NOT add idempotency check — see follow-up)

timeline.event: Sentry alert fired: duplicate refund event for charge ch_def456 (event b9d2c4e0)
Reinforced memory: agent triaged the same root-cause class 82s earlier (mem_reinforce_traceA_idempotency_cluster)
Reinforcement-surfaced DM: 'add a retry budget on idempotency keys' covers BOTH processCharge AND processRefund (mem_slk_dm_feb18_retry_budget)

root_cause.text: Same idempotency-check gap as the prior charge-side incident, but on the refund branch at line 91. The Feb 18 DM explicitly warned this would bite both processCharge and processRefund — the retry-budget memory surfaced because Trace A's reinforcement step made it hot. Same fix shape, different surface.

citations.excerpt: we should add a retry budget on idempotency keys, this'll bite us again — both processCharge AND processRefund use the same hot path and neither is wrapped
User triaged idempotency-key incident in webhooks/stripe.ts — reinforced this cluster. Reinforced memory_ids: [mem_slk_jan14_thread_001, mem_ntn_pm_2024_01_14, mem_ntn_adr_007].
ADR-007 — Idempotency keys for Stripe webhooks. lib/idempotency.ts.has() must be called before any side-effecting processCharge/processRefund.
async has(key: string): Promise<boolean> { const { rows } = await db.query("select 1 from webhook_events where event_id = $1 limit 1", [key]); return rows.length > 0; }
Every Stripe webhook handler MUST consult a persistent idempotency check on event.id before performing any side effect, on every code path including retry paths.

similar_incidents.summary: Feb 18 DM — predicted exactly this regression; covers refund branch too. NEW citation surfaced via Trace A's reinforcement.
Triage history — agent triaged the charge-side variant 82s earlier; same root-cause class.

ADR-007 — governs idempotency-key format; not enforced.
